files.Title: Xrpl.BinaryCodec.Types.AccountId
files.Summary:
Class defining how to encode and decode an AccountID
The unique identifier for an account.

files.Title: Xrpl.BinaryCodec.Types.Blob
files.Summary:
The Blob type is a length-prefixed field with arbitrary data.
Two common fields that use this type are SigningPubKey and TxnSignature, which contain (respectively) the public key and signature that authorize a transaction to be executed.
Blob fields have no further structure to their contents, so they consist of exactly the amount of bytes indicated in the variable-length encoding, after the Field ID and length prefixes.

Variable length encoded type

files.Title: Xrpl.BinaryCodec.Types.Currency
files.Summary: Class defining how to encode and decode Currencies

files.Title: Xrpl.BinaryCodec.Types.Hash
files.Summary:
The XRP Ledger has several "hash" types: Hash128, Hash160, and Hash256.
These fields contain arbitrary binary data of the given number of bits, which may or may not represent the result of a hash operation.
All such fields are serialized as the specific number of bits, with no length indicator, in big-endian byte order.

Base class defining how to encode and decode hashes

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Common.Currency
files.Summary:
The XRP Ledger has two kinds of digital asset: XRP and tokens.
Both types have high precision, although their formats are different

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Enums.LedgerEntryFilter
files.Summary: LedgerEntryFilter is used to filter the types of ledger entries returned by certain API calls (account_objects request).

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.AccountRootFlags
files.Summary:
There are several options which can be either enabled or disabled for an account.
These options can be changed with an AccountSet transaction.
In the ledger, flags are represented as binary values that can be combined with bitwise-or operations.
The bit values for the flags in the ledger are different than the values used to enable or disable those flags in a transaction.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.AuctionSlot
files.Summary: Details of the current owner of the auction slot.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.LOAccountRoot
files.Summary: The AccountRoot object type describes a single account, its settings, and XRP balance.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.LOAmendments
files.Summary: The Amendments object type contains a list of Amendments that are currently active.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.LOBaseLedger
files.Summary: base ledger fields

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.LOCheck
files.Summary: A Check object describes a check, similar to a paper personal check, which can be cashed by its destination to get money from its sender.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.LODepositPreauth
files.Summary:
A DepositPreauth object tracks a preauthorization from one account to another.
DepositPreauth transactions create these objects.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.LODirectoryNode
files.Summary: The DirectoryNode object type provides a list of links to other objects in the ledger's state tree.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.LOEscrow
files.Summary: The Escrow object type represents a held payment of XRP waiting to be executed or canceled.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.LOFeeSettings
files.Summary: The FeeSettings object type contains the current base transaction cost and reserve amounts as determined by fee voting.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.LOLedger
files.Summary: Response expected from a . https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl.js/blob/b20c05c3680d80344006d20c44b4ae1c3b0ffcac/packages/xrpl/src/models/methods/ledger.ts#L113

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.LOLedgerCurrentIndex
files.Summary: Response expected from a { LedgerCurrentRequest}.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.LOLedgerData
files.Summary: The response expected from a .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.LOLedgerHashes
files.Summary: The LedgerHashes objects exist to make it possible to look up a previous ledger's hash with only the current ledger version and at most one lookup of a previous ledger version.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.LOMPToken
files.Summary: The MPToken object represents an amount of an MPT held by an account that is not the issuer.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.LOMPTokenIssuance
files.Summary: The MPTokenIssuance object represents a Multi-Purpose Token (MPT) issuance.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.LONegativeUNL
files.Summary: The NegativeUNL object type contains the current status of the Negative UNL, a list of trusted validators currently believed to be offline.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.LOPayChannel
files.Summary:
The PayChannel object type represents a payment channel.
Payment channels enable small, rapid off-ledger payments of XRP that can be later reconciled with the consensus ledger.
A payment channel holds a balance of XRP that can only be paid out to a specific destination address until the channel is closed.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.LORippleState
files.Summary: The RippleState object type connects two accounts in a single currency.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.LOSignerList
files.Summary:
The SignerList object type represents a list of parties that, as a group, are authorized to sign a transaction in place of an individual account.
You can create, replace, or remove a signer list using a SignerListSet transaction.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.LOTicket
files.Summary:
The Ticket object type represents a Ticket, which tracks an account sequence number that has been set aside for future use.
You can create new tickets with a TicketCreate transaction.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.LedgerEntity
files.Summary:
A ledger is a block of transactions and shared state data.
It has a unique header that describes its contents using cryptographic hashes.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.LedgerIndexType
files.Summary:
The rippled server makes a distinction between ledger versions that are open, closed, and validated.
A server has one open ledger, any number of closed but unvalidated ledgers, and an immutable history of validated ledgers.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.QueuedTransaction
files.Summary:
Each member of the queue_data array represents one transaction in the queue.
Some fields of this object may be omitted because they have not yet been calculated.
https://xrpl.org/ledger.html#response-format

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.RippleStateFlags
files.Summary:
There are several options which can be either enabled or disabled for a trust line.
These options can be changed with a TrustSet transaction.
In the ledger, flags are represented as binary values that can be combined with bitwise-or operations.
The bit values for the flags in the ledger are different than the values used to enable or disable those flags in a transaction.
Ledger flags have names that begin with lsf.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Ledger.SignerEntry
files.Summary: The object that describes the signer in SignerEntries.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.LedgerEntryType
files.Summary:
Each ledger version's state data is a set of ledger objects, sometimes called ledger entries, which collectively represent all settings, balances, and relationships at a given point in time.
To store or retrieve an object in the state data, the protocol uses that object's unique Ledger Object ID.
In the peer protocol, ledger objects have a canonical binary format.In rippled APIs, ledger objects are represented as JSON objects.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.AMMInfoRequest
files.Summary: The amm_info command retrieves information about an AMM instance.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.AMMInfoResponse
files.Summary: Response expected from an .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.AccountChannels
files.Summary: The expected response from an .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.AccountChannelsRequest
files.Summary:
The account_channels method returns information about an account's Payment Channels.
This includes only channels where the specified account is the channel's source, not the destination.
(A channel's "source" and "owner" are the same.)
All information retrieved is relative to a particular version of the ledger.
Returns an .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.AccountCurrencies
files.Summary: The expected response from an .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.AccountCurrenciesRequest
files.Summary: The account_currencies command retrieves a list of currencies that an account can send or receive, based on its trust lines. Expects an

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.AccountInfo
files.Summary: Response expected from an .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.AccountInfoRequest
files.Summary:
The account_info command retrieves information about an account, its activity, and its XRP balance.
All information retrieved is relative to a particular version of the ledger. Returns an .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.AccountLines
files.Summary: Response expected from an .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.AccountLinesRequest
files.Summary:
The account_lines method returns information about an account's trust lines, including balances in all non-XRP currencies and assets.
All information retrieved is relative to a particular version of the ledger.
Expects an .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.AccountNFTs
files.Summary: Response expected from an .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.AccountNFTsRequest
files.Summary: The account_nfts method retrieves all of the NFTs currently owned by the specified account.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.AccountObjects
files.Summary: Response expected from an .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.AccountObjectsRequest
files.Summary:
The account_objects command returns the raw ledger format for all objects owned by an account.
For a higher-level view of an account's trust lines and balances, see the account_lines method instead.
Expects a response in the form of an .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.AccountOffers
files.Summary: Response expected from an form an .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.AccountOffersRequest
files.Summary:
The account_offers method retrieves a list of offers made by a given account that are outstanding as of a particular ledger version.
Expects a response in the form of a .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.AccountQueueData
files.Summary:
Information about queued transactions sent by account.
This information describes the state of the local rippled server, which may be different from other servers in the peer-to-peer XRP Ledger network.
Some fields may be omitted because the values are calculated "lazily" by the queuing mechanism.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.AccountQueueTransaction
files.Summary: Information about each queued transaction from address.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.AccountTransactions
files.Summary: Expected response from an .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.AccountTransactionsRequest
files.Summary:
The account_tx method retrieves a list of transactions that involved the specified account.
Expects a response in the form of a .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.AccountingStateSummary
files.Summary:
A map of various server states with information about the time the server spends in each.
This can be useful for tracking the long-term health of your server's connectivity to the network.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.Book
files.Summary: Defines the order book for monitoring updates

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.BookCurrency
files.Summary: Order book currency

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.BookOffersRequest
files.Summary:
The book_offers method retrieves a list of offers, also known as the order.
Book, between two currencies.
Returns an .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.Channel
files.Summary: Payment channel owned by account. https://xrpl.org/account_channels.html

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.ChannelAuthorizeRequest
files.Summary:
The channel_authorize method creates a signature that can be used to redeem a specific amount of XRP from a payment channel.
The request must specify exactly one of secret, seed, seed_hex, or passphrase. *** Warning: Do not send secret keys to untrusted servers or through unsecured network connections. ***
(This includes the secret, seed, seed_hex, or passphrase fields of this request.))
You should only use this method on a secure, encrypted network connection to a server you run or fully trust with your funds.
Otherwise, eavesdroppers could use your secret key to sign claims and take all the money from this payment channel and anything else using the same key pair.
See Set Up Secure Signing for instructions.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.ChannelVerifyRequest
files.Summary:
The channel_verify method checks the validity of a signature that can be used to redeem a specific amount of XRP from a payment channel.
Expects a response in the form of a ChannelVerifyResponse.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.Drops
files.Summary: The transaction cost

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.FeatureInfo
files.Summary:
Describes a single XRPL amendment (feature).

Depending on rippled version and node mode (validator / non-validator), the server may return either a minimal or an extended set of fields. This model is designed to be forward-compatible.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.Fee
files.Summary: Response expected from a .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.FeeRequest
files.Summary:
The fee command reports the current state of the open-ledger requirements for the transaction cost.
This requires the FeeEscalation amendment to be enabled.
Expects a response in the form of a .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.GatewayBalances
files.Summary: Expected response from a .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.GatewayBalancesRequest
files.Summary:
The gateway_balances command calculates the total balances issued by a given account, optionally excluding amounts held by operational addresses.
Expects a response in the form of a .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.JobType
files.Summary: (Admin only) Detailed information about the current load state of the server.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.LastClose
files.Summary: Information about the last time the server closed a ledger, including the amount of time it took to reach a consensus and the number of trusted validators participating.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.LedgerClosedRequest
files.Summary:
The ledger_closed method returns the unique identifiers of the most recently closed ledger.
Expects a response in the form of a .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.LedgerCurrentRequest
files.Summary:
The ledger_current method returns the unique identifiers of the current in-progress ledger.
Expects a response in the form of a

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.LedgerDataRequest
files.Summary:
The ledger_data method retrieves contents of the specified ledger.
You can iterate through several calls to retrieve the entire contents of a single ledger version.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.LedgerEntryRequest
files.Summary:
The ledger_entry method returns a single ledger object from the XRP Ledger in its raw format.
Expects a response in the form of a .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.LedgerRequest
files.Summary:
Retrieve information about the public ledger.
Expects a response in the form.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.LedgerStream
files.Summary:
The ledger stream only sends ledgerClosed messages when the consensus process declares a new validated ledger.
The message identifies the ledger and provides some information about its contents. https://xrpl.org/subscribe.html#ledger-stream

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.LedgerStreamResponse
files.Summary: This response mirrors the LedgerStream, except it does NOT include the 'type' nor 'txn_count' fields.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.Levels
files.Summary: required transaction cost level

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.Load
files.Summary: (Admin only) Detailed information about the current load state of the server.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.MPTokenQuery
files.Summary:
Object specifying the MPToken object to retrieve.
The mpt_issuance_id and account sub-fields are required.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.NFT
files.Summary: One NFToken that might be returned from an . https://xrpl.org/account_nfts.html#account_nfts

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.NFTBuyOffers
files.Summary: Response expected from an ..

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.NFTBuyOffersRequest
files.Summary: The nft_buy_offers method retrieves all of buy offers for the specified NFToken.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.NFTSellOffers
files.Summary: Response expected from an .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.NFTSellOffersRequest
files.Summary: The nft_sell_offers method retrieves all of sell offers for the specified NFToken.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.NoRippleCheck
files.Summary: Response expected by a .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.NoRippleCheckRequest
files.Summary:
The noripple_check command provides a quick way to check the status of th default ripple field for an account and the No Ripple flag of its trust lines, compared with the recommended settings.
Expects a response in the form of an { NoRippleCheckResponse}.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.Offer
files.Summary: offer made by account that is outstanding as of the requested ledger version.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.Path
files.Summary:
A path set is an array.
Each member of the path set is another array that represents an individual path.
Each member of a path is an object that specifies the step.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.PingRequest
files.Summary:
The ping command returns an acknowledgement, so that clients can test the connection status and latency.
Expects a response in the form of a PingRequest.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.RandomRequest
files.Summary:
The random command provides a random number to be used as a source of entropy for random number generation by clients.
Expects a response in the form of a RandomResponse.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.RippleState
files.Summary:
Object specifying the RippleState (trust line) object to retrieve.
The accounts and currency sub-fields are required to uniquely specify the rippleState entry to retrieve.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.RoleType
files.Summary:
Whether the address refers to a gateway or user.
Recommendations depend on the role of the account.
Issuers must have Default Ripple enabled and must disable No Ripple on all trust lines.
Users should have Default Ripple disabled, and should enable No Ripple on all trust lines.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.ServerInfoRequest
files.Summary:
The server_info command asks the server for a human-readable version of various information about the rippled server being queried.
Expects a response in the form of a { ServerInfoResponse}.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.ServerStateInner
files.Summary:
Depending on how the rippled server is configured, how long it has been running, and other factors, a server may be participating in the global XRP Ledger peer-to-peer network to different degrees.
This is represented as the server_state field in the responses to the server_info method and server_state method.
The possible responses follow a range of ascending interaction, with each later value superseding the previous one.
https://xrpl.org/rippled-server-states.html

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.ServerStateRequest
files.Summary:
The server_info command asks the server for a human-readable version of various information about the rippled server being queried.
Expects a response in the form of a { ServerInfoResponse}.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.SimulateRequest
files.Summary:
The simulate method executes a dry run of any transaction type, enabling you to preview the results and metadata of a transaction without committing them to the XRP Ledger.
Since this command never submits a transaction to the network, it doesn't incur any fees.
Expects a response in the form of a .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.StateLedger
files.Summary: Information about the most recent fully-validated ledger.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.SubmitRequest
files.Summary:
The submit method applies a transaction and sends it to the network to be confirmed and included in future ledgers.
Expects a response in the form of a .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.SubscribeRequest
files.Summary:
The subscribe method requests periodic notifications from the server when certain events happen.
https://xrpl.org/subscribe.html

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.TrustLine
files.Summary: Trust line objects.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.TxRequest
files.Summary:
The tx method retrieves information on a single transaction, by its identifying hash.
Expects a response in the form of a TxResponse.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.UnsubscribeRequest
files.Summary:
The unsubscribe command tells the server to stop sending messages for a particular subscription or set of subscriptions.
The parameters in the request are specified almost exactly like the parameters to the subscribe method, except that they are used to define which subscriptions to end instead. The rt_accounts and url parameters, and the rt_transactions stream name, are deprecated and may be removed without further notice.
The response follows the standard format, with a successful result containing no fields.
https://xrpl.org/unsubscribe.html

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.ValidatedLedger
files.Summary: Information about the most recent fully-validated ledger.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Methods.ValidationStream
files.Summary: The validations stream sends messages whenever it receives validation messages, also called validation votes, regardless of whether or not the validation message is from a trusted validator. https://xrpl.org/subscribe.html#validations-stream

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Subscriptions.AlternativePath
files.Summary: object with suggested paths to take, as described below.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Subscriptions.ConsensusStream
files.Summary:
The consensus stream sends consensusPhase messages when the consensus process changes phase.
The message contains the new phase of consensus the server is in. https://xrpl.org/subscribe.html#consensus-stream

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Subscriptions.OrderBookStream
files.Summary: When you subscribe to one or more order books with the books field, you get back any transactions that affect those order books. https://xrpl.org/subscribe.html#order-book-streams

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Subscriptions.PeerStatusStream
files.Summary: The admin-only peer_status stream reports a large amount of information on the activities of other rippled servers to which this server is connected, in particular their status in the consensus process. https://xrpl.org/subscribe.html#peer-status-stream

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Subscriptions.RippleResponseWarning
files.Summary: When the response contains a warnings array, each member of the array represents a separate warning from the server.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Subscriptions.TransactionStream
files.Summary:
Many subscriptions result in messages about transactions, including the following: The transactions stream
The transactions_proposed stream
accounts subscriptions
accounts_proposed subscriptions
book (Order Book) subscriptions https://xrpl.org/subscribe.html#transaction-streams

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.TransactionType
files.Summary:
The type of a transaction (TransactionType field) is the most fundamental information about a transaction.
This indicates what type of operation the transaction is supposed to do.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.AMMBid
files.Summary: AMMBid is used for submitting a vote for the trading fee of an AMM Instance. Any XRPL account that holds LPToken for an AMM instance may submit this transaction to vote for the trading fee for that instance.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.AMMBidResponse
files.Summary: AMMBid is used for submitting a vote for the trading fee of an AMM Instance. Any XRPL account that holds LPToken for an AMM instance may submit this transaction to vote for the trading fee for that instance.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.AMMCreate
files.Summary: AMMCreate is used to create AccountRoot and the corresponding AMM ledger entries. This allows for the creation of only one AMM instance per unique asset pair.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.AMMCreateResponse
files.Summary: AMMCreate is used to create AccountRoot and the corresponding AMM ledger entries. This allows for the creation of only one AMM instance per unique asset pair.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.AMMDelete
files.Summary: Delete an empty Automated Market Maker (AMM) instance that could not be fully deleted automatically. Tip: The AMMWithdraw transaction automatically tries to delete an AMM, along with associated ledger entries such as empty trust lines, if it withdrew all the assets from the AMM's pool. However, if there are too many trust lines to the AMM account to remove in one transaction, it may stop before fully removing the AMM.Similarly, an AMMDelete transaction removes up to a maximum number of trust lines; in extreme cases, it may take several AMMDelete transactions to fully delete the trust lines and the associated AMM. In all cases, the AMM ledger entry and AMM account are deleted by the last such transaction.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.AMMDeleteResponse
files.Summary: Delete an empty Automated Market Maker (AMM) instance that could not be fully deleted automatically. Tip: The AMMWithdraw transaction automatically tries to delete an AMM, along with associated ledger entries such as empty trust lines, if it withdrew all the assets from the AMM's pool. However, if there are too many trust lines to the AMM account to remove in one transaction, it may stop before fully removing the AMM.Similarly, an AMMDelete transaction removes up to a maximum number of trust lines; in extreme cases, it may take several AMMDelete transactions to fully delete the trust lines and the associated AMM. In all cases, the AMM ledger entry and AMM account are deleted by the last such transaction.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.AMMDeposit
files.Summary:
AMMDeposit is the deposit transaction used to add liquidity to the AMM instance pool, thus obtaining some share of the instance's pools in the form of LPTokenOut. The following are the recommended valid combinations:

  • LPTokenOut
  • Amount
  • Amount and Amount2
  • Amount and LPTokenOut
  • Amount and EPrice

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.AMMDepositFlags
files.Summary: Enum representing values for AMMDeposit Transaction Flags.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.AMMDepositResponse
files.Summary:
AMMDeposit is the deposit transaction used to add liquidity to the AMM instance pool, thus obtaining some share of the instance's pools in the form of LPTokenOut. The following are the recommended valid combinations:

  • LPTokenOut
  • Amount
  • Amount and Amount2
  • Amount and LPTokenOut
  • Amount and EPrice

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.AMMWithdraw
files.Summary: AMMWithdraw is the withdraw transaction used to remove liquidity from the AMM instance pool, thus redeeming some share of the pools that one owns in the form of LPTokenIn.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.AMMWithdrawFlags
files.Summary: Enum representing values for AMMWithdrawFlags Transaction Flags

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.AMMWithdrawResponse
files.Summary: AMMWithdraw is the withdraw transaction used to remove liquidity from the AMM instance pool, thus redeeming some share of the pools that one owns in the form of LPTokenIn.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.AccountDelete
files.Summary: An AccountDelete transaction deletes an account and any objects it owns in the XRP Ledger, if possible, sending the account's remaining XRP to a specified destination account.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.AccountDeleteResponse
files.Summary: An AccountDelete transaction deletes an account and any objects it owns in the XRP Ledger, if possible, sending the account's remaining XRP to a specified destination account.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.AccountSet
files.Summary: An AccountSet transaction modifies the properties of an account in the XRP Ledger.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.AccountSetAsfFlags
files.Summary: Enum for AccountSet Flags.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.AccountSetResponse
files.Summary: An AccountSet transaction modifies the properties of an account in the XRP Ledger.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.AffectedNode
files.Summary: The AffectedNodes array contains a complete list of the objects in the ledger that this transaction modified in some way.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.BaseTransactionResponse
files.Summary: Supports all classes in the .NET class hierarchy and provides low-level services to derived classes. This is the ultimate base class of all .NET classes; it is the root of the type hierarchy.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.BookOffers
files.Summary: • Expected response from a .

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.CheckCancel
files.Summary:
Cancels an unredeemed Check, removing it from the ledger without sending any money.
The source or the destination of the check can cancel a Check at any time using this transaction type.
If the Check has expired, any address can cancel it.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.CheckCancelResponse
files.Summary:
Cancels an unredeemed Check, removing it from the ledger without sending any money.
The source or the destination of the check can cancel a Check at any time using this transaction type.
If the Check has expired, any address can cancel it.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.CheckCash
files.Summary:
Attempts to redeem a Check object in the ledger to receive up to the amount authorized by the corresponding CheckCreate transaction.
Only the Destination address of a Check can cash it with a CheckCash transaction.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.CheckCashResponse
files.Summary:
Attempts to redeem a Check object in the ledger to receive up to the amount authorized by the corresponding CheckCreate transaction.
Only the Destination address of a Check can cash it with a CheckCash transaction.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.CheckCreate
files.Summary:
Create a Check object in the ledger, which is a deferred payment that can be cashed by its intended destination.
The sender of this transaction is the sender of the Check.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.CheckCreateResponse
files.Summary:
Create a Check object in the ledger, which is a deferred payment that can be cashed by its intended destination.
The sender of this transaction is the sender of the Check.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.ClawBack
files.Summary: The Clawback transaction is used by the token issuer to claw back issued tokens from a holder.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.ClawBackResponse
files.Summary: ClawBack is used for submitting a vote for the trading fee of an AMM Instance. Any XRPL account that holds LPToken for an AMM instance may submit this transaction to vote for the trading fee for that instance.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.CreatedNode
files.Summary: Represents a node that was created in a transaction.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.DeletedNode
files.Summary: Represents a node that was deleted in a transaction.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.DepositPreauth
files.Summary:
A DepositPreauth transaction gives another account pre-approval to deliver payments to the sender of this transaction.
This is only useful if the sender of this transaction is using (or plans to use) Deposit Authorization.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.DepositPreauthResponse
files.Summary:
A DepositPreauth transaction gives another account pre-approval to deliver payments to the sender of this transaction.
This is only useful if the sender of this transaction is using (or plans to use) Deposit Authorization.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.EscrowCancel
files.Summary: Return escrowed XRP to the sender.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.EscrowCancelResponse
files.Summary: Return escrowed XRP to the sender.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.EscrowCreate
files.Summary: Sequester XRP until the escrow process either finishes or is canceled.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.EscrowCreateResponse
files.Summary: Sequester XRP until the escrow process either finishes or is canceled.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.EscrowFinish
files.Summary: Deliver XRP from a held payment to the recipient.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.EscrowFinishResponse
files.Summary: Deliver XRP from a held payment to the recipient.

Tip: The AMMWithdraw transaction automatically tries to delete an AMM, along with associated ledger entries such as empty trust lines, if it withdrew all the assets from the AMM's pool. However, if there are too many trust lines to the AMM account to remove in one transaction, it may stop before fully removing the AMM.Similarly, an AMMDelete transaction removes up to a maximum number of trust lines; in extreme cases, it may take several AMMDelete transactions to fully delete the trust lines and the associated AMM. In all cases, the AMM ledger entry and AMM account are deleted by the last such transaction.

  • LPTokenOut
  • Amount
  • Amount and Amount2
  • Amount and LPTokenOut

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.IBaseTransactionResponse
files.Summary:
This information is added to Transactions in request responses, but is not part of the canonical Transaction information on ledger.
These fields are denoted with lowercase letters to indicate this in the rippled responses.

files.Summary:
Cancels an unredeemed Check, removing it from the ledger without sending any money.
The source or the destination of the check can cancel a Check at any time using this transaction type.
If the Check has expired, any address can cancel it.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.ICheckCash
files.Summary:
Attempts to redeem a Check object in the ledger to receive up to the amount authorized by the corresponding CheckCreate transaction.
Only the Destination address of a Check can cash it with a CheckCash transaction.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.ICheckCreate
files.Summary:
Create a Check object in the ledger, which is a deferred payment that can be cashed by its intended destination.
The sender of this transaction is the sender of the Check.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.IClawBack
files.Summary: ClawBack is used for submitting a vote for the trading fee of an AMM Instance. Any XRPL account that holds LPToken for an AMM instance may submit this transaction to vote for the trading fee for that instance.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.IDepositPreauth
files.Summary:
A DepositPreauth transaction gives another account pre-approval to deliver payments to the sender of this transaction.
This is only useful if the sender of this transaction is using (or plans to use) Deposit Authorization.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.IDestination
files.Summary: object has destination field

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.IEscrowCancel
files.Summary: Return escrowed XRP to the sender.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.IEscrowCreate
files.Summary: Sequester XRP until the escrow process either finishes or is canceled.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.IEscrowFinish
files.Summary: Deliver XRP from a held payment to the recipient.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.IMPTokenAuthorize
files.Summary: The MPTokenAuthorize transaction is used to allow an account to hold a particular MPT issuance, or by an issuer to authorize or revoke authorization for a holder.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.IMPTokenIssuanceCreate
files.Summary: The MPTokenIssuanceCreate transaction creates an MPTokenIssuance object and adds it to the relevant directory node of the creator account.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.IMPTokenIssuanceDestroy
files.Summary: The MPTokenIssuanceDestroy transaction is used to remove an MPTokenIssuance object from the directory node in which it is being held, effectively removing the token from the ledger.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.IMPTokenIssuanceSet
files.Summary: The MPTokenIssuanceSet transaction is used to globally lock/unlock an MPTokenIssuance, or to lock/unlock a specific holder's MPToken balance for an MPTokenIssuance.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.INFTokenAcceptOffer
files.Summary:
The NFTokenOfferAccept transaction is used to accept offers to buy or sell an NFToken.
It can either:
1.
Allow one offer to be accepted.
This is called direct mode.
2.
Allow two distinct offers, one offering to buy a given NFToken and the other offering to sell the same NFToken, to be accepted in an atomic fashion.
This is called brokered mode.
To indicate direct mode, use either the sell_offer or buy_offer fields, but not both.
To indicate brokered mode, use both the sell_offer and buy_offer fields.
If you use neither sell_offer nor buy_offer, the transaction is invalid.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.INFTokenBurn
files.Summary:
The NFTokenBurn transaction is used to remove an NFToken object from the NFTokenPage in which it is being held, effectively removing the token from the ledger ("burning" it).
If this operation succeeds, the corresponding NFToken is removed.
If this operation empties the NFTokenPage holding the NFToken or results in the consolidation, thus removing an NFTokenPage, the owner’s reserve requirement is reduced by one.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.INFTokenCancelOffer
files.Summary:
The NFTokenCancelOffer transaction deletes existing NFTokenOffer objects.
It is useful if you want to free up space on your account to lower your reserve requirement.
The transaction can be executed by the account that originally created the NFTokenOffer, the account in the Recipient field of the NFTokenOffer (if present), or any account if the NFTokenOffer has an Expiration and the NFTokenOffer has already expired.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.INFTokenCreateOffer
files.Summary: The NFTokenCreateOffer transaction creates either an offer to buy an NFT the submitting account does not own, or an offer to sell an NFT the submitting account does own.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.INFTokenMint
files.Summary:
The NFTokenMint transaction creates an NFToken object and adds it to the relevant NFTokenPage object of the minter.
If the transaction is successful, the newly minted token will be owned by the minter account specified by the transaction.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.INFTokenModify
files.Summary:
NFTokenModify is used to change the URI field of an NFT to point to a different URI in order to update the supporting data for the NFT.
The NFT must have been minted with the tfMutable flag set.
See Dynamic Non-Fungible Tokens.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.IOfferCancel
files.Summary: An OfferCancel transaction removes an Offer object from the XRP Ledger.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.IOfferCreate
files.Summary:
An OfferCreate transaction is effectively a limit order.
It defines an intent to exchange currencies, and creates an Offer object if not completely.
Fulfilled when placed.
Offers can be partially fulfilled.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.IPayment
files.Summary: A Payment transaction represents a transfer of value from one account to another.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.IPaymentChannelClaim
files.Summary: Claim XRP from a payment channel, adjust the payment channel's expiration, or both.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.IPaymentChannelCreate
files.Summary:
Create a unidirectional channel and fund it with XRP.
The address sending this transaction becomes the "source address" of the payment channel.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.IPaymentChannelFund
files.Summary:
Add additional XRP to an open payment channel, and optionally update the expiration time of the channel.
Only the source address of the channel can use this transaction.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.ISetRegularKey
files.Summary: A SetRegularKey transaction assigns, changes, or removes the regular key pair associated with an account.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.ISignerListSet
files.Summary: The SignerListSet transaction creates, replaces, or removes a list of signers that can be used to multi-sign a transaction.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.ITicketCreate
files.Summary: A TicketCreate transaction sets aside one or more sequence numbers as Tickets.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.ITransactionCommon
files.Summary: Every transaction has the same set of common fields.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.ITransactionResponse
files.Summary: Every transaction has the same set of common fields.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.ITrustSet
files.Summary: Create or modify a trust line linking two accounts.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.InnerSigner
files.Summary: The Signers field contains a multi-signature, which has signatures from up to 8 key pairs, that together should authorize the transaction.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.MPTokenAuthorize
files.Summary: The MPTokenAuthorize transaction authorizes an account to hold an MPT.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.MPTokenAuthorizeFlags
files.Summary: Enum representing flags for MPTokenAuthorize transactions.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.MPTokenAuthorizeResponse
files.Summary: The MPTokenAuthorize transaction is used to allow an account to hold a particular MPT issuance, or by an issuer to authorize or revoke authorization for a holder.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.MPTokenIssuanceCreate
files.Summary: The MPTokenIssuanceCreate transaction creates an MPTokenIssuance object.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.MPTokenIssuanceCreateFlags
files.Summary: Enum representing flags for MPTokenIssuanceCreate transactions.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.MPTokenIssuanceCreateResponse
files.Summary: The MPTokenIssuanceCreate transaction creates an MPTokenIssuance object and adds it to the relevant directory node of the creator account.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.MPTokenIssuanceDestroy
files.Summary: The MPTokenIssuanceDestroy transaction removes an MPTokenIssuance object.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.MPTokenIssuanceDestroyResponse
files.Summary: The MPTokenIssuanceDestroy transaction is used to remove an MPTokenIssuance object from the directory node in which it is being held, effectively removing the token from the ledger.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.MPTokenIssuanceSet
files.Summary: The MPTokenIssuanceSet transaction is used to globally lock/unlock an MPTokenIssuance.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.MPTokenIssuanceSetFlags
files.Summary: Enum representing flags for MPTokenIssuanceSet transactions.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.MPTokenIssuanceSetResponse
files.Summary: The MPTokenIssuanceSet transaction is used to globally lock/unlock an MPTokenIssuance, or to lock/unlock a specific holder's MPToken balance for an MPTokenIssuance.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.Memo
files.Summary: The Memos field includes arbitrary messaging data with the transaction.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.MemoWrapper
files.Summary: Additional arbitrary information used to identify transaction.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.Meta
files.Summary:
Transaction metadata is a section of data that gets added to a transaction after it is processed.
Any transaction that gets included in a ledger has metadata, regardless of whether it is successful.
The transaction metadata describes the outcome of the transaction in detail.
Warning: The changes described in transaction metadata are only final if the transaction is in a validated ledger version.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.ModifiedNode
files.Summary: Represents a node that was modified in a transaction.

files.Summary:
The NFTokenOfferAccept transaction is used to accept offers to buy or sell an NFToken.
It can either:
1.

This is called direct mode.

Allow two distinct offers, one offering to buy a given NFToken and the other offering to sell the same NFToken, to be accepted in an atomic fashion.

To indicate direct mode, use either the sell_offer or buy_offer fields, but not both.

If you use neither sell_offer nor buy_offer, the transaction is invalid.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.Offer
files.Summary:
The Offer object type describes an offer to exchange currencies, more traditionally known as an order, in the XRP Ledger's distributed exchange.
An OfferCreate transaction only creates an Offer object in the ledger when the offer cannot be fully executed immediately by consuming other offers already in the ledger.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.OfferCancel
files.Summary: An OfferCancel transaction removes an Offer object from the XRP Ledger.

files.Summary:
An OfferCreate transaction is effectively a limit order.
It defines an intent to exchange currencies, and creates an Offer object if not completely.
Fulfilled when placed.
Offers can be partially fulfilled.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.OfferCreateFlags
files.Summary: Transaction Flags for an OfferCreate Transaction.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.OfferCreateResponse
files.Summary:
An OfferCreate transaction is effectively a limit order.
It defines an intent to exchange currencies, and creates an Offer object if not completely.
Fulfilled when placed.
Offers can be partially fulfilled.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.OfferFlags
files.Summary:
There are several options which can be either enabled or disabled when an OfferCreate transaction creates an offer object.
In the ledger, flags are represented as binary values that can be combined with bitwise-or operations.
The bit values for the flags in the ledger are different than the values used to enable or disable those flags in a transaction.
Ledger flags have names that begin with lsf.

Create a unidirectional channel and fund it with XRP.
The address sending this transaction becomes the "source address" of the payment channel.

Create a unidirectional channel and fund it with XRP.
The address sending this transaction becomes the "source address" of the payment channel.

files.Title: Xrpl.Models.Transactions.PaymentChannelFund

Add additional XRP to an open payment channel, and optionally update the expiration time of the channel.
Only the source address of the channel can use this transaction.

Add additional XRP to an open payment channel, and optionally update the expiration time of the channel.
Only the source address of the channel can use this transaction.

files.Title: Xrpl.Utils.Hashes.HashPrefix
files.Summary:
Prefix for hashing functions.
These prefixes are inserted before the source material used to generate various hashes.This is done to put each hash in its own "space."
This way, two different types of objects with the same binary data will produce different hashes.
Each prefix is a 4-byte value with the last byte set to zero and the first three bytes formed from the ASCII equivalent of some arbitrary string.
For example "TXN".

files.Title: Xrpl.Utils.Hashes.LedgerSpace
files.Summary: XRP Ledger namespace prefixes. The XRP Ledger is a key-value store.In order to avoid name collisions, names are partitioned into namespaces. Each namespace is just a single character prefix. SeeLedgerNameSpace enum.
